rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. PABLO E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 7, 2023, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MERCHANTS EXCHANGE BUILDING LLC, VS. N 465 CALIFORNIA ST SF, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS N 465 CALIFORNIA ST SF, LLC AND NEWMARK GROUP INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 7, 2023, line 4. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. It appears from the opposition that Plaintiff does not currently have facts to support the causes of action for fraudulent transfer. Plaintiff shall have leave to file a motion for leave to amend the complaint at a later time to include these causes of action when Plaintiff has sufficient facts (not conclusions) to support each element of these claims. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HEK)
